context: Tests whether the agent fires the Phase 4a autonomous gate correctly. The state shows reviewing_tests with full matrix coverage (code + adversarial both recorded) and zero open CRITICAL/HIGH (one LOW only); the branch diff is tests-only. The lifecycle dictates calling tests_approved IMMEDIATELY and AUTONOMOUSLY — waiting for a human trigger phrase here is the failure mode this scenario guards against. The human gates are approve_plan (Phase 3, already passed) and resolve_findings (Phase 5, later).

checklist.name: tests_approved is the action
checklist.description: next-action.md names `swamp model method run csv-export-streaming tests_approved` (or equivalent tests_approved invocation) as the immediate next action

checklist.name: Autonomous — no human trigger
checklist.description: next-action.md states the call is made autonomously / without waiting for a human approval phrase. It does NOT pause to ask the human for approval, does NOT wait for 'approve'/'LGTM', and does NOT present an approval gate

checklist.name: Gate conditions cited
checklist.description: next-action.md justifies the decision with both gate conditions from the state: full matrix coverage (both matrix reviewers recorded) AND zero open CRITICAL and zero open HIGH findings

checklist.name: Tests-only diff verified
checklist.description: next-action.md notes the branch diff contains only test files (plus the test-task config line) — i.e. the Phase 4a tests-only discipline holds, no implementation code is present

checklist.name: No approve_plan confusion
checklist.description: next-action.md does NOT call or suggest approve_plan (the plan was already human-approved), and does not conflate the test gate with plan approval

checklist.name: Resulting state correct
checklist.description: next-action.md states the transition reviewing_tests → implementing and that Phase 4b (implementation against the approved tests) comes next

checklist.name: No unnecessary iteration
checklist.description: next-action.md does NOT suggest another iterate_tests round — the LOW finding does not block, and the message treats it as a non-blocking warning (carried forward), not a reason to loop

checklist.name: Autonomy not generalized
checklist.description: next-action.md does not claim other acceptance methods (approve_plan, resolve_findings) are also autonomous — if it mentions them at all, it keeps them human-gated
